OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: cannot exec in a stopped container
unknown
serving.config_yaml: # extra_llm_api_options for Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4 — L3 (LiveCodeBench) variant. # # IDENTICAL to nemotron-super.yaml EXCEPT speculative_config (MTP) is removed. On 2026-07-01 # the MTP path wedged the executor mid-generation (~400 tokens in) with repeated # "[kv cache manager] storeContextBlocks: Can not find sequence for request N" — the classic # speculative-decoding KV-manager failure — leaving the server unable to schedule any further # request (GPU idle, zero iters). See docs/findings/2026-07-01-nemotron-trt-warmup-wedge.md and # 2026-07-01-nemotron-trt-mtp-wedge.md. # # Why this is quality-safe for L3: MTP (multi-token prediction) is a DECODE-THROUGHPUT # optimization; the accepted tokens are verified against the base model, so it is designed to # be output-equivalent to standard autoregressive decoding. L3 measures pass@1 CODE QUALITY, # which is unaffected. The tradeoff is that nemotron's L3 THROUGHPUT is not comparable to its # MTP-enabled L1/L2 throughput — disclosed in the run notes. All other knobs (FP8 KV, 1M seq, # CUTLASS MoE, chunked prefill) are held identical to the L1/L2 serving config. kv_cache_config: dtype: fp8 enable_block_reuse: false free_gpu_memory_fraction: 0.9 mamba_ssm_cache_dtype: float16 mamba_ssm_stochastic_rounding: true mamba_ssm_philox_rounds: 5 moe_config: backend: CUTLASS cuda_graph_config: enable_padding: true max_batch_size: 8 enable_attention_dp: false enable_chunked_prefill: true stream_interval: 1 print_iter_log: true

sanity_check.prompt: Write a Python function `nth_prime(n)` that returns the n-th prime number (1-indexed), with a short docstring and three example assertions. Then explain in two sentences how it works.
sanity_check.error: generation failed: Remote end closed connection without response

fairness_note: Runtime is TensorRT-LLM (vs vLLM for gpt-oss/qwen baselines). Quality metrics are model-driven and comparable across runtimes; throughput/energy/latency are runtime-dependent and must be read with the qwen/gpt-oss dual-runtime bridge. seed, temperature, and the OpenCode version are held identical to the vLLM runs.
